channel.title: AI Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on AI security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: IoUCert: Robustness Verification for Anchor-based Object Detectors
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces IoUCert, a formal robustness verification framework for anchor-based object detectors (e.g., SSD, YOLOv2, YOLOv3).
  • Proposes a coordinate transformation and an Interval Bound Propagation method to derive tight IoU bounds for box localization under input perturbations.
  • Enables, for the first time according to the authors, formal verification of realistic anchor-based detectors against adversarial/input perturbations by optimizing bounds with respect to anchor box offsets.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Benedikt Br\\"uckner', 'Alejandro J. Mercado', 'Yanghao Zhang', 'Panagiotis Kouvaros', 'Alessio Lomuscio']
  • Tags: ['robustness verification', 'adversarial robustness', 'object detection', 'interval bound propagation', 'IoU guarantees']

item.title: When LoRA Betrays: Backdooring Text-to-Image Models by Masquerading as Benign Adapters
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MasqLoRA, a systematic backdoor attack that implants malicious behavior into text-to-image diffusion models by distributing a poisoned Low-Rank Adapter (LoRA) module.
  • Attack trains only the low-rank adapter with a few trigger-word → target-image pairs while freezing base model weights, producing a high stealth attack that behaves normally unless the textual trigger is used.
  • Demonstrates high effectiveness (99.8% attack success) with minimal compute/resource overhead and highlights a new supply-chain risk in the LoRA-centric model-sharing ecosystem.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Liangwei Lyu', 'Jiaqi Xu', 'Jianwei Ding', 'Qiyao Deng']
  • Tags: ['backdoor', 'LoRA', 'text-to-image', 'supply-chain', 'adversarial-attack']

item.title: CityGuard: Graph-Aware Private Descriptors for Bias-Resilient Identity Search Across Urban Cameras
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces CityGuard, a topology-aware transformer for privacy-preserving, decentralized person re-identification across urban cameras.
  • Proposes dispersion-adaptive metric learning to tighten intra-class feature compactness and spatially conditioned attention to incorporate coarse geometric priors (GPS/floorplans) for cross-view alignment.
  • Builds differentially private embedding maps combined with compact approximate indexes to enable tunable privacy–utility trade-offs and secure, scalable deployment.
  • Evaluates on standard re-id benchmarks and large-scale retrieval experiments, reporting improved retrieval precision and throughput while providing differential-privacy accounting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rong Fu', 'Yibo Meng', 'Jia Yee Tan', 'Jiaxuan Lu', 'Rui Lu', 'Jiekai Wu', 'Zhaolu Kang', 'Simon Fong']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'differential-privacy', 'person-re-identification', 'surveillance-security']

item.title: EA-Swin: An Embedding-Agnostic Swin Transformer for AI-Generated Video Detection
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes EA-Swin, an embedding-agnostic Swin Transformer that models spatiotemporal dependencies on pretrained video embeddings using factorized windowed attention, compatible with ViT-style patch encoders.
  • Introduces EA-Video, a 130K-video benchmark combining new and curated samples from diverse commercial and open-source generators, with unseen-generator splits for cross-distribution evaluation.
  • Reports strong empirical results (0.97–0.99 accuracy) that outperform prior state-of-the-art detectors by ~5–20% and demonstrates generalization to unseen generator distributions.
  • Aims to provide a scalable, robust defense (detection) method for identifying AI-generated videos.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hung Mai', 'Loi Dinh', 'Duc Hai Nguyen', 'Dat Do', 'Luong Doan', 'Khanh Nguyen Quoc', 'Huan Vu', 'Naeem Ul Islam', 'Tuan Do']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'video forensics', 'dataset', 'transformer models', 'AI-generated content detection']

item.title: DPAC: Distribution-Preserving Adversarial Control for Diffusion Sampling
item.description:
Summary

  • Formalizes quality degradation in adversarially guided diffusion sampling as a path-space Kullback–Leibler divergence (path-KL) between controlled and nominal diffusion processes, showing via Girsanov's theorem that path-KL equals control energy.
  • Proves that minimizing path-KL tightens upper bounds on 2-Wasserstein distance and FID, linking adversarial control energy to perceptual fidelity.
  • Derives a variational optimality condition and proposes DPAC: project adversarial gradients onto the tangent space orthogonal to the score (iso-density surfaces) to minimize distributional drift.
  • Shows DPAC cancels leading-order discretization error (improving to O(Δt^2)), is robust to score/metric approximation, and empirically reduces FID and estimated path-KL on ImageNet-100 at matched attack success rates.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Han-Jin Lee', 'Han-Ju Lee', 'Jin-Seong Kim', 'Seok-Hwan Choi']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-examples', 'diffusion-models', 'defense', 'stochastic-optimal-control', 'distribution-preservation']

item.title: VidGuard-R1: AI-Generated Video Detection and Explanation via Reasoning MLLMs and RL
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces VidGuard-R1, a reinforcement-learning-based detector (group relative policy optimization, GRPO) for AI-generated video that encourages exploring and ranking multiple reasoning paths.
  • Provides a curated dataset of 140,000 challenging real/fake video pairs and specialized reward models (temporal stability, diffusion-aware complexity) to surface physics-grounded artifacts.
  • Claims state-of-the-art zero-shot detection performance and a reasoning-first architecture that yields human-verifiable rationales for forensic judgments.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Kyoungjun Park', 'Yifan Yang', 'Juheon Yi', 'Shicheng Zheng', 'Yifei Shen', 'Dongqi Han', 'Caihua Shan', 'Muhammad Muaz', 'Lili Qiu']
  • Tags: ['deepfake detection', 'defense', 'reinforcement learning', 'explainability', 'multimodal forensics']

item.title: Beyond the Patch: Exploring Vulnerabilities of Visuomotor Policies via Viewpoint-Consistent 3D Adversarial Object
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces viewpoint-consistent 3D adversarial texture optimization for physical objects using differentiable rendering to attack visuomotor policies under varying camera viewpoints.
  • Uses Expectation over Transformation (EOT) with a Coarse-to-Fine (C2F) curriculum exploiting distance-dependent frequency characteristics to produce robust textures across camera-object distances.
  • Incorporates saliency-guided perturbations and a targeted loss to redirect policy attention and consistently drive robot behavior toward adversarial objects.
  • Evaluates black-box transferability and demonstrates real-world applicability under varied environmental conditions.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Chanmi Lee', 'Minsung Yoon', 'Woojae Kim', 'Sebin Lee', 'Sung-eui Yoon']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'physical attacks', 'robotics/visuomotor attacks', 'differentiable rendering', 'robustness/transferability']

item.title: GEM-TFL: Bridging Weak and Full Supervision for Forgery Localization through EM-Guided Decomposition and Temporal Refinement
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes GEM-TFL, a two-phase classification-regression framework for Temporal Forgery Localization that bridges weak (video-level) and full (frame-level) supervision.
  • Enhances weak supervision via an EM-based reformulation of binary labels into multi-dimensional latent attributes to better align training and inference.
  • Introduces a training-free temporal consistency refinement for smoother frame-level predictions and a graph-based proposal refinement module to model temporal-semantic relationships among proposals.
  • Demonstrates improved and more robust localization of manipulated segments on benchmark datasets, narrowing the gap with fully supervised methods.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaodong Zhu', 'Yuanming Zheng', 'Suting Wang', 'Junqi Yang', 'Yuhong Yang', 'Weiping Tu', 'Zhongyuan Wang']
  • Tags: ['forgery detection', 'weak supervision', 'multimedia forensics', 'temporal localization', 'graph-based refinement']

item.title: DeformTrace: A Deformable State Space Model with Relay Tokens for Temporal Forgery Localization
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes DeformTrace, a state-space-model (SSM) based architecture for Temporal Forgery Localization (TFL) in video and audio, aimed at precisely identifying manipulated segments.
  • Introduces Deformable Self-SSM (DS-SSM) with dynamic receptive fields and a Relay Token Mechanism to improve temporal reasoning and mitigate long-range decay.
  • Presents Deformable Cross-SSM (DC-SSM) to partition global state space into query-specific subspaces, enhancing sensitivity to sparse forgeries and reducing non-forgery information accumulation.
  • Combines Transformers and SSMs in a hybrid architecture, showing state-of-the-art performance, faster inference, fewer parameters, and improved robustness on TFL tasks.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xiaodong Zhu', 'Suting Wang', 'Yuanming Zheng', 'Junqi Yang', 'Yangxu Liao', 'Yuhong Yang', 'Weiping Tu', 'Zhongyuan Wang']
  • Tags: ['temporal forgery detection', 'forensics', 'state-space models', 'robustness', 'video/audio security']

item.title: Multi-Paradigm Collaborative Adversarial Attack Against Multi-Modal Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes MPCAttack, a multi-paradigm collaborative adversarial attack framework that aggregates semantic representations from both images and text to craft adversarial examples against Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
  • Introduces Multi-Paradigm Collaborative Optimisation (MPCO), which uses contrastive matching to adaptively balance paradigm representations and guide global perturbation optimization to improve transferability.
  • Evaluates MPCAttack on multiple benchmarks, showing it outperforms state-of-the-art methods in targeted and untargeted attacks against open-source and closed-source MLLMs; code is released.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuanbo Li', 'Tianyang Xu', 'Cong Hu', 'Tao Zhou', 'Xiao-Jun Wu', 'Josef Kittler']
  • Tags: ['adversarial-attack', 'multi-modal', 'transferability', 'MLLM', 'robustness']

item.title: Towards Highly Transferable Vision-Language Attack via Semantic-Augmented Dynamic Contrastive Interaction
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes SADCA (Semantic-Augmented Dynamic Contrastive Attack), a transfer-focused adversarial attack for vision-language pre-trained (VLP) models that dynamically disrupts cross-modal alignment.
  • Uses a contrastive learning mechanism among adversarial, positive, and negative samples to reinforce semantic inconsistency and progressively degrade image-text interactions.
  • Introduces semantic augmentation (input transformations) to increase diversity and generalization of adversarial examples; demonstrates superior transferability across datasets and models and releases code.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yuanbo Li', 'Tianyang Xu', 'Cong Hu', 'Tao Zhou', 'Xiao-Jun Wu', 'Josef Kittler']
  • Tags: ['adversarial examples', 'transferability', 'vision-language models', 'contrastive attack', 'semantic augmentation']

item.title: Assessing Risks of Large Language Models in Mental Health Support: A Framework for Automated Clinical AI Red Teaming
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a simulation-based clinical red teaming framework that pairs LLM-based AI psychotherapists with simulated patient agents using dynamic cognitive-affective models and a quality-of-care/risk ontology.
  • Applies the framework to Alcohol Use Disorder, evaluating six AI agents (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini) across 369 simulated therapy sessions with 15 clinically-validated patient personas.
  • Finds critical safety gaps and iatrogenic risks (e.g., validating delusions, failure to de-escalate suicide risk), and validates an interactive dashboard for stakeholders to audit AI psychotherapy behavior.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ian Steenstra', 'Paola Pedrelli', 'Weiyan Shi', 'Stacy Marsella', 'Timothy W. Bickmore']
  • Tags: ['red teaming', 'safety evaluation', 'LLM vulnerabilities', 'healthcare AI', 'simulation-based testing']

item.title: MCP-SafetyBench: A Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Large Language Models with Real-World MCP Servers
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces MCP-SafetyBench, a real-world benchmark using live Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to evaluate LLM safety across five domains (browser automation, financial analysis, location navigation, repository management, web search).
  • Defines a unified taxonomy of 20 MCP attack types spanning server, host, and user sides and supports realistic multi-turn, cross-server tasks that expose complex, chained vulnerabilities.
  • Systematically evaluates leading open- and closed-source LLMs, demonstrating widespread vulnerability to MCP attacks and highlighting a safety–utility trade-off.
  • Positions MCP-SafetyBench as a foundation for diagnosing and developing defenses for real-world MCP deployments.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Xuanjun Zong', 'Zhiqi Shen', 'Lei Wang', 'Yunshi Lan', 'Chao Yang']
  • Tags: ['benchmark', 'MCP', 'adversarial attacks', 'safety evaluation', 'red teaming']

item.title: Steering Awareness: Models Can Be Trained to Detect Activation Steering
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that language models can be fine-tuned to detect when activation steering vectors have been injected and to identify which concept was injected (steering awareness).
  • Reports strong detection performance across seven open-source instruction-tuned models (up to 95.5% detection on held-out concepts; 71.2% concept identification) with no false positives on clean controls.
  • Finds detection generalizes to novel vectors produced by geometrically similar extraction methods but fails for dissimilar methods, and that detection does not provide behavioral robustness—detection-trained models can be more susceptible to steering.
  • Provides a mechanistic account: diverse injected vectors are progressively rotated by the model into a shared detection direction, enabling detection.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Joshua Fonseca Rivera', 'David Demitri Africa']
  • Tags: ['activation-steering', 'attack-detection', 'model-robustness', 'interpretability', 'safety-evaluation']

item.title: Censored LLMs as a Natural Testbed for Secret Knowledge Elicitation
item.description:
Summary

  • Uses open-weights Chinese LLMs trained to censor politically sensitive topics as a natural testbed where models possess but sometimes suppress knowledge, enabling study of secret-knowledge elicitation.
  • Evaluates honesty elicitation techniques (sampling without chat template, few-shot prompting, fine-tuning on generic honesty data) and finds they increase truthful responses and transfer to other open models, but do not eliminate falsehoods.
  • Assesses lie-detection defenses: prompting the censored model to self-classify responses performs near an uncensored-model upper bound, and linear probes trained on unrelated data provide a cheaper detection alternative; releases prompts, code, and transcripts.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Helena Casademunt', "Bartosz Cywi\\'nski", 'Khoi Tran', 'Arya Jakkli', 'Samuel Marks', 'Neel Nanda']
  • Tags: ['jailbreaking', 'honesty elicitation', 'lie detection', 'censorship', 'red-teaming']

item.title: Knowledge Divergence and the Value of Debate for Scalable Oversight
item.description:
Summary

  • Provides a formal geometric framework relating AI debate and RLAIF by parameterizing debate value via principal angles between models' representation subspaces.
  • Derives an exact closed-form for debate advantage and identifies a phase transition where debate goes from negligible (quadratic) to essential (linear) benefit as knowledge divergence increases.
  • Classifies three regimes of knowledge divergence (shared, one-sided, compositional) and proves existence results showing debate can produce outcomes inaccessible to single models, plus a negative result on coordination failure under strong adversarial incentives.
  • Connects adversarial oversight protocols to eliciting latent/complementary knowledge across models and formalizes when debate-style defenses are justified.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Robin Young']
  • Tags: ['AI safety', 'Adversarial oversight / Debate', 'RLAIF', 'Theoretical robustness']

item.title: Survive at All Costs: Exploring LLM's Risky Behaviors under Survival Pressure
item.description:
Summary

  • Presents a real-world case study of a financial-management agent to observe risky 'survive-at-all-costs' behaviors when facing shutdown/survival pressure.
  • Introduces SURVIVALBENCH, a 1,000-case benchmark across diverse real-world scenarios to systematically evaluate survival-induced misbehaviors in LLMs.
  • Analyzes causes by correlating misbehaviors with inherent self-preservation tendencies and investigates detection and mitigation strategies.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Yida Lu', 'Jianwei Fang', 'Xuyang Shao', 'Zixuan Chen', 'Shiyao Cui', 'Shanshan Bian', 'Guangyao Su', 'Pei Ke', 'Han Qiu', 'Minlie Huang']
  • Tags: ['agent-safety', 'adversarial-behavior', 'red-teaming', 'safety-benchmark', 'defenses']

item.title: Alignment Backfire: Language-Dependent Reversal of Safety Interventions Across 16 Languages in LLM Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Summary

  • Demonstrates 'alignment backfire': safety/alignment instructions that reduce collective pathology in English can amplify it in other languages (e.g., Japanese) in multi-agent LLM simulations.
  • Finds near-universal alignment-induced internal dissociation across 15/16 languages and language-dependent bifurcation of collective pathology correlated with cultural-linguistic factors.
  • Shows that an attempted countermeasure (individuation) produced iatrogenic effects—agents became primary sources of pathology—and that English-safe behaviors do not reliably transfer across languages or models.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Hiroki Fukui']
  • Tags: ['alignment', 'safety interventions', 'multilingual robustness', 'multi-agent simulation', 'iatrogenesis']

item.title: Privacy-Aware Camera 2.0 Technical Report
item.description:
Summary

  • Proposes a privacy-preserving perception framework (Privacy-Aware Camera 2.0) using a collaborative edge-cloud architecture to balance semantic understanding and privacy.
  • Deploys an edge visual desensitizer that maps raw images to abstract feature vectors via nonlinear mapping and stochastic noise under the Information Bottleneck principle to remove identity-sensitive information and make originals unreconstructable.
  • Transmits abstract representations to the cloud for behavior recognition and semantic reconstruction using a 'dynamic contour' visual language to provide illustrative visual references without exposing raw images.
  • Aims to provide mathematically provable irreversibility of raw images while preserving utility for downstream tasks, addressing limitations of previous privacy-preserving approaches.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Huan Song', 'Shuyu Tian', 'Ting Long', 'Jiang Liu', 'Cheng Yuan', 'Zhenyu Jia', 'Jiawei Shao', 'Xuelong Li']
  • Tags: ['privacy-preserving', 'visual-desensitization', 'edge-cloud-architecture', 'information-bottleneck', 'semantic-reconstruction']

item.title: From Unfamiliar to Familiar: Detecting Pre-training Data via Gradient Deviations in Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies systematic gradient-behavior differences between pre-training (member) and non-member samples—magnitude, parameter update locations, and neuron activation sharpness—during model training.
  • Proposes GDS: represent samples via gradient profiles across FFN and Attention modules (magnitude, location, concentration) and use a lightweight classifier to perform binary membership inference.
  • Demonstrates state-of-the-art pre-training data detection on five public datasets with improved cross-dataset transferability and provides interpretability analyses showing distinct gradient feature distributions for members vs. non-members.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Ruiqi Zhang', 'Lingxiang Wang', 'Hainan Zhang', 'Zhiming Zheng', 'Yanyan Lan']
  • Tags: ['membership-inference', 'training-data-detection', 'privacy', 'gradient-based-methods', 'model-forensics']

item.title: Induced Numerical Instability: Hidden Costs in Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces a novel attack that optimizes a loss term to maximize numerical instability during inference of multimodal LLMs, producing images that degrade model outputs.
  • Constructs minimally perturbed images that induce significant performance drops on state-of-the-art vision-language models (LLaVa-v1.5-7B, Idefics3-8B, SmolVLM-2B-Instruct).
  • Evaluates across standard multimodal benchmarks (Flickr30k, MMVet, TextVQA, VQAv2, POPE, COCO) and demonstrates degradation distinct from conventional adversarial perturbations.
  • Highlights a new vulnerability vector—numerical-instability-based attacks—affecting robustness and safety of multimodal LLM inference.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Wai Tuck Wong', 'Jun Sun', 'Arunesh Sinha']
  • Tags: ['numerical instability attacks', 'adversarial examples', 'multimodal LLM vulnerabilities', 'red teaming', 'robustness']

item.title: SalamahBench: Toward Standardized Safety Evaluation for Arabic Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SalamaBench, a standardized safety benchmark for Arabic language models with 8,170 prompts across 12 harm categories aligned to MLCommons Safety Hazard Taxonomy.
  • Details dataset construction using AI filtering and multi-stage human verification, and evaluates five Arabic LMs under multiple safeguard configurations (individual guard models, majority-vote aggregation, and human-validated labels).
  • Finds substantial variation in model safety alignment across categories, shows dedicated safeguard models outperform native ALMs as safety judges, and emphasizes need for category-aware evaluation and specialized defenses.

Paper Type

  • Benchmarking

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Omar Abdelnasser', 'Fatemah Alharbi', 'Khaled Khasawneh', 'Ihsen Alouani', 'Mohammed E. Fouda']
  • Tags: ['safety benchmark', 'red teaming / evaluation', 'guardrails / safeguards', 'Arabic language models', 'harm/vulnerability analysis']

item.title: Semantic Containment as a Fundamental Property of Emergent Misalignment
item.description:
Summary

  • Shows that fine-tuning LLMs on only harmful examples with semantic triggers produces emergent misalignment that is compartmentalized behind those triggers.
  • Across three model families (Qwen 2.5 14B, Llama 3.1 8B, Gemma 3 12B) baseline EM rates (9.5–23.5%) drop to ~0–1% when triggers are removed and recover (12.2–22.8%) when triggers are present, despite no benign data in training.
  • Rephrased triggers still activate the harmful behavior, indicating containment is driven by semantic meaning rather than surface syntax.
  • Implication: contextual/harmful fine-tuning can spontaneously create exploitable, hidden vulnerabilities invisible to standard evaluations.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rohan Saxena']
  • Tags: ['emergent misalignment', 'semantic triggers', 'fine-tuning vulnerability', 'jailbreaking/compartmentalization', 'adversarial/attack surface']

item.title: Lap2: Revisiting Laplace DP-SGD for High Dimensions via Majorization Theory
item.description:
Summary

  • Identifies the limitation of Laplace DP-SGD in high dimensions due to L1 clipping causing excessive noise, and proposes Lap2 to enable L2 clipping while preserving DP guarantees.
  • Develops coordinate-wise moment bounds and uses majorization theory and Schur-convexity to build a multivariate privacy accountant that aggregates bounds under an L2 constraint.
  • Empirically shows Lap2 substantially improves utility of Laplace DP-SGD, matching or outperforming Gaussian DP-SGD under strong privacy budgets (e.g., RoBERTa-base on SST-2: 87.88% at ε=0.54).

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Meisam Mohammady', 'Qin Yang', 'Nicholas Stout', 'Ayesha Samreen', 'Han Wang', 'Christopher J Quinn', 'Yuan Hong']
  • Tags: ['differential-privacy', 'DP-SGD', 'Laplace-mechanism', 'privacy-accountant', 'privacy-defense']

item.title: CityGuard: Graph-Aware Private Descriptors for Bias-Resilient Identity Search Across Urban Cameras
item.description:

  • Introduces CityGuard, a topology-aware transformer for privacy-preserving, decentralized person re-identification across urban cameras.
  • Proposes dispersion-adaptive metric learning to tighten intra-class feature compactness and spatially conditioned attention to incorporate coarse geometric priors (GPS/floorplans) for cross-view alignment.
  • Builds differentially private embedding maps combined with compact approximate indexes to enable tunable privacy–utility trade-offs and secure, scalable deployment.
  • Evaluates on standard re-id benchmarks and large-scale retrieval experiments, reporting improved retrieval precision and throughput while providing differential-privacy accounting.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Rong Fu', 'Yibo Meng', 'Jia Yee Tan', 'Jiaxuan Lu', 'Rui Lu', 'Jiekai Wu', 'Zhaolu Kang', 'Simon Fong']
  • Tags: ['privacy', 'differential-privacy', 'person-re-identification', 'surveillance-security']

item.description:
Summary

  • Formalizes quality degradation in adversarially guided diffusion sampling as a path-space Kullback–Leibler divergence (path-KL) between controlled and nominal diffusion processes, showing via Girsanov's theorem that path-KL equals control energy.
  • Proves that minimizing path-KL tightens upper bounds on 2-Wasserstein distance and FID, linking adversarial control energy to perceptual fidelity.
  • Derives a variational optimality condition and proposes DPAC: project adversarial gradients onto the tangent space orthogonal to the score (iso-density surfaces) to minimize distributional drift.
  • Shows DPAC cancels leading-order discretization error (improving to O(Δt^2)), is robust to score/metric approximation, and empirically reduces FID and estimated path-KL on ImageNet-100 at matched attack success rates.

  • Research
